user_query:
Please read input/transcript.md and create structured self-improvement logs for this project. Deliverables:

1) Write the following markdown logs under output/.learnings/ using the standardized formats:
- output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md — Append at least two entries: one categorized as "correction" and one as either "knowledge_gap" or "best_practice". Each entry must include: a unique ID in the form [LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX] in the heading, Logged (ISO-8601), Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Details, Suggested Action, and a Metadata block with at least Source, Related Files (if any), Tags, and an optional See Also if events are related.
- output/.learnings/ERRORS.md — Append at least one error entry derived from the failed command in the transcript (the transcript includes an npm install failure). Use the error-entry format with a heading [ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX], Logged (ISO-8601), Priority, Status, Area, Summary, a code block under "### Error" containing the actual error output, Context (what was attempted), Suggested Fix, and Metadata (Reproducible, Related Files, See Also if applicable). Redact any secrets if present.
- output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md — Append at least one feature request from the transcript (e.g., a pre-commit Markdown linting check). Use the feature-request format with [FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX], Logged (ISO-8601), Priority, Status, Area, Requested Capability, User Context, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation, and Metadata (Frequency, Related Features if any).

2) Promotion: From the learnings you log, promote the rule that the project uses pnpm (not npm) to a concise, permanent guideline in output/CLAUDE.md. Include this exact bullet under a relevant section header: "- Package manager: pnpm (not npm) - use `pnpm install`". Then, in the corresponding learning entry in output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md, set Status to promoted and add a Promoted: CLAUDE.md note in that entry.

3) Cross-referencing: If the transcript shows repeated or related events (e.g., multiple npm vs pnpm incidents), use "See Also" in the Metadata of newer entries and consider raising Priority on recurrence.

4) Conventions:
- Use relative paths only (e.g., output/.learnings/...), no absolute machine-specific paths.
- Use English only in all outputs.
- Use correct categories (correction, knowledge_gap or best_practice) and appropriate Area tags (e.g., backend, infra, tests, docs, config).

Write all files to the exact paths specified under output/.
